calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate (C.C.P. Section 1085), Declaratory And Injunctive Relief; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Tracy Mccray And Stephen Giacalone And Exhibits In Support Thereto
Off Calendar Jan-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Richard Levine, Esq. 925-609-1699 Rains Lucia Stern St. Phalle for Plaintiff.(via Zoom)
Derek Ulmer, Esq. 916-851-1900 Goyette Ruano & Thompson for Plaintiff/Petitioner. (via Zoom)
Tara Steeley, Esq.415-554-4655 Co-chief of litigation and Carmen Leon, Esq. 415-554-3966, Deputy City Attorney for Defendant CCSF. (in-person)
Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977, sheilapham28@gmail.com, Reported. (via Zoom)

Argued. The Court inquires on the late contest email. Mr. Levine responds. No one objects to Mr. Levine arguing. Both sides argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF IAFF SAN FRANCISCO FIREFIGHTERS UNION LOCAL 798, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION's Hearing For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate (C.C.P. Section 1085), Declaratory And Injunctive Relief.

The "petition for writ of mandate, declaratory and injunctive relief" is stayed under the court's inherent powers. (OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141.)

In June 2021, the City and County of San Francisco adopted a Covid-19 vaccination requirement for its employees - a requirement that no longer exists. Petitioners - police and fire unions - filed their writ action in November 2021, but then failed to pursue it for two years.

Petitioner San Francisco Police Officers Association also filed an unfair practices complaint with the Public Employment Relations Board that has some of the same issues as the writ action, most notably back pay. This court's stay is effective until PERB proceedings are final. (Doyle v. City of Chino (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 673, 681 ["Judicial review by way of writ of mandate is generally foreclosed until all administrative remedies have been exhausted."].)

Whether the writ action is moot, as the City asserts, can be resolved, if necessary, after the stay is lifted on noticed motion. In the interim, the parties are encouraged to resolve whatever of their differences remain live.

Order is signed.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: RIchard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977, sheilapham28@gmail.com, Reported. (302)

Notice Of Hearing For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate (C.C.P. Section 1085), Declaratory And Injunctive Relief; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Tracy Mccray And Stephen Giacalone And Exhibits In Support Thereto
Off Calendar Dec-05-2023 Continued To Jan-09-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Richard Levine, Esq. 310-393-1486 Raines Lucia Stern St. Phalle & Silver PC for Plaintiff.
Derek Ulmer, Esq. 916-851-1900 Goyette Ruano & Thompson for Plaintiff. (via Zoom)
Carmen Leon, Esq. 415-554-3800, Deputy Cty Attorney, Tara M. Steeley, Esq. 415-554-4700 Co-Chief of appellate litigation, City Attrney's Office for Defendant CCSF.
Court reporter: Cheri Bullock, CSR #4714, 661-645-4048, cheridbullock@gmail.com, reported. (via Zoom)

Hearing is held. The Court states he needs ALL the papers in compliance with Local Rule 2.7(B). The Court inquires on administrative record. Mr. Levine responds. Counsel for CCSF informs the court of a related PURB case. Mr. Levine responds. Mr. Levine hands a hard copy of his papers to the calendar clerk.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS IAFF SAN FRANCISCO FIREFIGHTERS UNION LOCAL 798, and SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION's Hearing For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate (C.C.P. Section 1085), Declaratory And Injunctive Relief.

Continued to 1/9/24 at 9:30am. Parties may file a 5-page, double-space supplemental brief re the issue of stay or delay by 12/15/23.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. , Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Cheri Bullock, CSR #4714, 661-645-4048, cheridbullock@gmail.com, reported. (302/RBU)
